svg.text: toolint — will a model actually use these tools correctly?
$ toolint server.tools.json
server.tools.json
error tool-description-placeholder description "TODO" is unfinished placeholder text
↳ the model will read this literally; replace it before shipping the server
error default-mismatch parameter "mode" defaults to "quick", which is not one of its enum values
warn enum-vague parameter "mode" has vague enum values: "option1", "option2"
↳ name what each value does ("fast_draft", "high_quality"), not its position in a list
warn boolean-negated boolean "no_cache" is phrased as a negation — "no_cache": false is a double negative
error name-generic name "run" tells the model nothing about what this tool does
↳ name the action and its object, e.g. "run_sql_query" instead of "run"
warn tool-description-short description "Search documents" is only 2 words — too thin to disambiguate…
error name-collision name "doc_search" collides with "DocSearch" (tool #2) — they differ only in case…
↳ models treat these as interchangeable; merge the tools or rename one to state its distinct action
✖ 27 problems (8 errors, 19 warnings) in 4 of 4 tools
$ toolint --format json server.tools.json | jq .summary
{ "errors": 8, "warnings": 19, "infos": 0, "tools": 4, "problems": 27 }
$ toolint fixed.tools.json && echo ready to ship
✔ 4 tools clean ready to ship
34 model-usability rules · MCP + OpenAI + Claude shapes · CI exit codes · offline · zero dependencies
